text: Online Registration and Fee Payment 1) Student will receive an email and SMS regarding the registration credentials in your personal email ID and mobile number as shared by JoSAA on 22 July 2025. 2) Student can login to the portal using those credentials. 3) Login Screen: Click on Student Login and enter login credentials 4) Personal Details: After logging in, My Details link will be seen in home page. Click on My Details link to view and edit personal details Click on Name to edit the record and update personal details 5) Download and Uploading of documents: You can download the template document which needs to uploaded by the student while registration process Downloading of templates: Click on forward Arrow icon to view templates and download Uploading of documents: Click on name to edit the record and click on Clip icon to go to attachments uploading tab and upload documents against each document. 6) Hostel Form: Click on Hostel Form link and click Add New to enter details, after entering, click on insert to submit details. 7) Qualifications: Click on Qualifications link and click Add New to enter qualification details. 8) Submitting Details and Documents: Click on submit after uploading and entering Qualifications and Hostel Declaration details. However, if there is any observation while document verification process it will be sent back with remarks for correction and fee challan will not be created for the payment. Fee Challan link will be seen on Home Page after verifying Documents. Click on the link to View Challans. Click on Fee receipt to view component wise amounts. Payment Link: Click on Pay link to pay the fee online, after clicking on Pay link, a pop-up for confirmation will be seen. After verifying amount, click on Submit to pay. text: General Guidelines for the freshers (First year UG students) Dear Students and Parents, Warm greetings and welcome to IIT Jammu! As you prepare to join the Institute, kindly take note of the following important guidelines to ensure a smooth and comfortable reporting process: 1. Accommodation for Parents/Guardians: On-campus accommodation for accompanying parents/guardians will not be provided. Families are requested to make suitable arrangements at hotels/guest houses etc in Jammu. 2. Campus Exit Restrictions Post-Reporting: Once a student has formally reported and completed check-in at the hostel, leaving the campus will not be permitted if not accompanied with parents until the student ID card is issued, which may take some time. This is to ensure safety and administrative compliance. 3. Special Provision for Accompanied Students: Students arriving with their parents may be permitted to stay overnight on 27th July 2025 only at their respective accommodation outside the campus. This provision is applicable only if the student is accompanied by their parent(s). To avail this, a written application must be submitted in person by the parent(s) to the respective Hostel authorities for record. The application will then be forwarded to the Main Gate, Security Office for necessary intimation. Students must report back to campus without fail on the morning of 28th July 2025. 4. Vehicle Restrictions: As per directions from the state authorities, vehicle movement is strictly restricted between 12:00 midnight and 07:00 AM due to the ongoing Amarnath Yatra. These restrictions are being rigorously enforced at the designated check-posts, specially at the check-post on NH-44 (near Sidhra). In view of the above, all students are advised not to travel towards the Institute during this restricted period. If you happen to arrive in Jammu during these hours, you are advised to stay at the Railway Station or any other safe location until the restrictions are lifted. 5. Essential Supplies: Vendor stalls will be set up at the hostels for essential items such as mattresses, pillows, buckets, and other daily-use items. However, students are free to bring these items with them or purchase them from outside, as per their convenience. 6. Mobile Connectivity: Kindly note that prepaid mobile SIM cards from outside J&K do not work in Jammu. Students are advised to either carry a postpaid SIM card or plan to purchase a new SIM locally upon arrival. 7. Use of Electrical Devices: Only essential devices such as mobile phones & laptops are

Mess Charges: To be paid at the time of admission in online mode before deadline as given in point 2 above. Academic Fees: Summary of the Academic Fees to be paid by the students of various categories is given below: SC/ST/PwD Category admitted ⇒ Fees particulars⤵ Total Academic Fee for the 1st semester of Preparatory Rs. 44,970/- 2024 batch Balance JOSAA Seat Acceptance Fees minus Processing Charges by JOSAA: This fee is to be paid or might have already been paid by the students in the JOSAA web portal to secure a seat. This fee will be deducted from the total Academic Fees to be paid to the institute. The receipt of payment of JOSAA Seat Rs. 15,000-5,000 Acceptance Fees is mandatory for the deduction. = 10,000/- Note that Rs. 5,000/- is the uniform Processing Charges by JOSAA irrespective of the category of the student. Net Academic Fees to be paid to the Institute = [Total Academic Fees – (JoSAA Seat Acceptance Fee - Processing Charge by JOSAA) Rs. 34,970/- Note: Fee components other than tuition fees will increase annually at the rate of 7%. Example: Let us consider that for a student, the Total Academic Fees as decided by the institute is Rs. 44,970/-. Let us also consider that the student has already paid Rs.15,000/- towards JoSAA Seat Acceptance Fee, and processing charges deducted by JoSAA are 5,000/-. Therefore, at the time of admission to the institute, the student has to pay the following as Net Academic Fees: [Rs. 44,970 - (15,000 - 5,000)] = Rs. 34,970/-]. For details on fee structure kindly visit: https://www.iitjammu.ac.in/fee For payment of fees, candidates may avail Net Banking or Debit card / Credit card facilities. Regarding the aggregate marks of 75% (or 65% for SC, ST and PwD) · The aggregate marks scored by the candidate in the 2026 Class XII (or equivalent) Board examination will be considered for candidates who will appear in the Class XII examination in 2026. · The aggregate marks scored by the candidate in the 2025 Class XII (or equivalent) Board examination will be considered provided the candidate does not reappear in the Class XII (or equivalent) examination in 2025 in any of the subjects. · If a candidate had appeared in his/her Class XII (or equivalent) in 2025 and wishes to improve his/her aggregate marks to meet the “aggregate marks of 75% (or 65% for SC, ST and PwD) criterion”, he/she can reappear for any number of subjects he/she wishes for improvement. If s/he does so, then s/he has to refund the availed scholarship for that Semester. IIT Jammu started B. Tech. programme in the year 2016 with an initial intake of 30 students each in Computer Science and Engineering, Electrical Engineering and Mechanical Engineering discipline. In the year 2017, B. Tech. in Civil Engineering discipline started with an initial intake of 30 students. In the year 2018, B. Tech. in Chemical Engineering discipline started with an initial intake of 30 students. Starting with the 2019-20 session, B. Tech. in Materials Engineering was added with an initial intake of 30 students. IIT Jammu has updated seat intake in 2021 re-emphasizing our approach to inter-disciplinarity that is deeply rooted in our own Academic structure from 2019. Starting with the 2023-24 session, B. Tech. in Mathematics and Computing is added with an initial intake of 40 students. Starting with the 2025-26 session, B.Tech in Engineering Physics has started. IIT Jammu introduced B. Tech in Unified Engineering from the 11th admission session 2026-27.

Ample opportunities and support is extended to deserving students. IIT Jammu gives full fledged on campus experience.

text:
## UG Programme B.Tech

The following programmes are offered by IIT Jammu for undergraduate studies.

1. B.Tech. in Chemical Engineering
2. B.Tech. in Civil Engineering
3. B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering
4. B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering
5. B.Tech in Engineering Physics
6. B.Tech. in Materials and Metallurgical Engineering
7. B.Tech. in Mathematics and Computing
8. B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering
9. B.Tech. in Unified Engineering

**Note:**

1. **IIT Jammu** offers a dual degree undergraduate program in collaboration with **IIM Mumbai**, allowing students to earn both a B.Tech degree from IIT Jammu and an MBA in Operational and Supply Chain Management from IIM Mumbai within five years.
2. IIT Jammu UG programs include 20% seats (category-wise) for **IIT Jammu - IIM Jammu** dual degree program (four-year B.Tech of IIT Jammu and two-year MBA of IIM Jammu), transition to IIM Jammu MBA to be opted at the end of third year of the IIT Jammu B.Tech program. The transition to IIM Jammu MBA program is direct without CAT subject to having a minimum CGPA of 7.0 in the B.Tech program. Those not transitioning to the MBA program will get a B.Tech degree on completion of the academic requirements of IIT Jammu.

The admission to these 4-year B.Tech. programmes is done through Joint Entrance Examination - Advanced (JEE-Advanced) rank and administered by the Joint Admission Board (JAB) through centralized counseling conducted by JoSAA at the all-India level.
